rulings.Case Title: THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, VS. THERESA LYNNE MURPHY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities In Support Of Plaintiff Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2022 line 1. PLAINTIFF THE BANK OF NEW YORK MELLON, Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To Amend Complaint is DENIED without prejudice. Moving party failed to comply with CRC 3.1324. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: TONY CHI HUA VS. DAMES SHOUMAN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Quash Service Of Plaintiff'S Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2022 line 2. DEFENDANT JESSICA ESMAY MOTION / Notice Of Motion & Motion To Quash Service Of Plaintiff'S Complaint is continued to November 27, 2022. No later than October 21, 2022 moving party to file a supplemental declaration addressing the outcome of the probate proceedings. In the alternative, in light of the new information provided in the Plaintiff's supplemental declaration, including Plaintiff's belief that the correct owner of the Property is 2019 trust, Plaintiff may file a motion for leave to amend the complaint to add a new party to be heard before November 27, 2022. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: CHINA LIVE VENTURES LIMITED, L.P. VS. 644 BROADWAY LLC ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2022 line 3. DEFENDANT 644 BROADWAY LLC, A DELAWARE LIMITED LIABILITY MOTION TO STRIKE 3RD Amended COMPLAINT is GRANTED in part and DENIED in part. Motion is grated as to the request for special damages and equitable relief with leave to amend for Plaintiff to allege nature and amount of special damages as well as the nature of the equitable relief that is being sought. Motion is DENIED as to the request for punitive damages. Facts sufficiently stated to support malice and such damages are available in tort claims. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. rulings.Case Title: CLIFF FOGLE ET AL VS. ALEXANDERSON PROPERTIES ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel-
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2022 line 4. PLAINTIFF CLIFF FOGLE Notice Of Motion And Motion To Be Relieved As Counsel is GRANTED. rulings.Case Title: JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. THE ESTATE OF DIANA CARTER-BIBBS ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2022 line 5. DEFENDANT JERRY FUQUA DEMURRER to 3RD Amended COMPLAINT is SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Demurrer is sustained with leave to amend for Plaintiff to attach a complete legible copy of the Lease with any addenda/attachments in Plaintiff's possession or expressly allege that Plaintiff is not in possession of all or part of such document. Demurrer is otherwise overruled. Complaint states the requisite relationship between landlord-tenant-sub-tenant. Any arguments not appearing as grounds for the Demurrer in the Notice of Motion are not properly before the Court, and are therefore, overruled. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). rulings.Case Title: THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY VS. ASKAL EQUBASILASIE ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Motion And Notice Of Hearing For Terminating Sanctions And/Or Monetary Sanctions Against Yoseph Gebremeskel
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2022 line 7. PLAINTIFF THE JOHN STEWART COMPANY Plaintiff'S Motion And Notice Of Hearing For Terminating Sanctions And/Or Monetary Sanctions Against Yoseph Gebremeskel is DENIED, premature. Plaintiff seeks "strike the answer" of Yoseph Gebremeskel. On September 20, 2022 this Court sustained Plaintiff's demurrer to defendant's answer, and therefore there is no answer on file to be stricken. The Court notes that as of September 27, 2022 there is no notice of entry of order on the demurrer on file. The Court further notes that the Discovery Order, which was entered on September 2, 2022, has not been served on the Defendant until September 9, 2022 to be delivered on September 10, 2022 (Saturday) with a compliance date of September 12, 2022 (Monday). =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849). Parties who intend to appear at the hearing must give notice to opposing parties and the court promptly, but no later than 4:00 p.m. the court day before the hearing unless the tentative ruling has specified that a hearing is required. Notice of contesting a tentative ruling shall be provided by sending an email to the court to Department501ContestTR@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. A party may not argue at the hearing if the opposing party is not so notified, and the opposing party does not appear.

rulings.Case Title: LYNN MILAN VS. YORDAN K. NIKOLOV
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for September 30, 2022 line 8. DEFENDANT YORDAN NIKOLOV MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT is DENIED. Moving party failed to shift its burden and show that Defendant is entitled to judgment as a matter of law. Specifically, Defendant failed to show that SFRO 37.9(o) was in effect at the time of the service of the operative notice. =(501/REQ) Parties may appear in-person, telephonically or via Zoom (Video - Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849; or Phone Dial in: (551) 285-1373; Webinar ID: 160 560 5023; Password: 172849).
